skill.name: planning-with-files
skill.description: Implements Manus-style file-based planning for complex tasks. Creates task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md. Use when starting complex multi-step tasks, research projects, or any task requiring >5 tool calls.
skill.summary: Implements Manus-style file-based planning for complex tasks. Creates task_plan.md, findings.md, and...
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 101 potential issues but evaluation confirms all are FALSE POSITIVES. The detected external_commands are markdown documentation examples showing AI tool usage, not actual shell execution. Network access is limited to a single legitimate reference URL. Filesystem access is standard file operations. The skill implements Manus-style planning methodology with no malicious intent. Safe for publication.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands (Markdown Documentation Examples)
dangerous_patterns.description: 78 instances of shell command syntax in markdown files flagged as 'external_commands'. All are FALSE POSITIVES - these are documentation examples showing AI tool usage (Write, Read, Edit), NOT actual shell commands being executed. No dynamic command construction or user input injection present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are markdown code blocks containing documentation examples, not executable code. The scanner incorrectly identifies example commands in documentation as actual shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URL (Reference Link)
dangerous_patterns.description: URL at reference.md:218 flagged as 'hardcoded URL'. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the URL is a legitimate reference to the official Manus blog documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL https://manus.im/blog/Context-Engineering-for-AI-Agents-Lessons-from-Building-Manus is a citation to source material, not a security risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Storage Access (Documentation Example)
dangerous_patterns.description: Reference to 'localStorage' in examples.md flagged as browser storage access. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - it's a documentation example in markdown showing how to store theme preferences.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The localStorage reference appears in a documentation example within a markdown code block, not actual browser code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Critical Heuristic: Code Execution + Network + Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: Flagged as dangerous combination. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the skill is a legitimate planning methodology that uses file-based state management. Network access is only to reference documentation. No credential access exists.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The heuristic incorrectly flags this planning skill as dangerous. Analysis confirms no malicious intent - it's a standard task planning methodology.

content.value_statement: AI agents lose track of goals during long tasks. This skill uses persistent markdown files as external memory so agents can handle complex multi-step projects without losing context.
actual_capabilities: Creates task_plan.md for phase tracking and goal management
Creates findings.md to store research and discoveries
Creates progress.md for session logging and test results
Provides templates for consistent planning structure
Includes helper scripts for session initialization and completion checking
Implements the 3-strike error protocol for systematic problem solving
limitations: Does not execute code - only creates planning documentation
Requires manual invocation or detection of complex tasks
User must read plan files to maintain context awareness
Files must be created in the working project directory

use_cases.title: Research Projects
use_cases.description: Use task_plan.md to track research phases, findings.md to store discoveries, and progress.md to log session activity. Prevents losing track of sources and insights.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and researchers doing information gathering

use_cases.title: Bug Fixing
use_cases.description: Create a task plan with phases for diagnosis, root cause identification, fix implementation, and testing. Track errors encountered to avoid repeating failed approaches.

use_cases.title: Feature Development
use_cases.description: Plan feature implementation with phases, store design decisions in findings.md, and log progress throughout development. Maintains focus across many implementation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a task_plan.md file with phases for: [describe your task]. Use the planning-with-files skill structure.

prompt_templates.title: Research with Findings
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up planning files for a research task on [topic]. Create task_plan.md with phases, findings.md for storing sources, and progress.md for logging.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use planning-with-files to debug [issue]. Create task_plan.md with diagnosis phases, track errors in the plan, and document findings as you discover root cause.

prompt_templates.title: Full Project Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the complete planning-with-files workflow: create all three planning files, implement the 2-action rule, use the 3-strike error protocol, and apply read-before-decide pattern.

output_examples.input: Research the benefits of morning exercise and write a summary
output_examples.output: Created task_plan.md with phases: Create plan, Search sources, Synthesize findings, Deliver summary. Phase 1 complete. Ready to search for sources.

output_examples.input: Fix the login bug in the authentication module
output_examples.output: Created task_plan.md with phases: Understand bug, Locate code, Identify root cause, Implement fix, Test. Currently in Phase 3 - found root cause.

output_examples.input: Add dark mode toggle to settings page
output_examples.output: Created task_plan.md, findings.md with existing theme system analysis, and progress.md. Phase 1 complete - identified theme files to modify.

best_practices: Create task_plan.md BEFORE starting any complex task with more than 5 tool calls
Re-read the plan file before making major decisions to refresh goals in context
Update task_plan.md after each phase to track progress and mark completion status
anti_patterns: Using TodoWrite for persistence instead of creating task_plan.md files
Starting execution immediately without creating a plan file first
Hiding errors and retrying silently instead of logging to plan file

faq.question: Where should I create the planning files?
faq.answer: Create task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md in your project directory, not in the skill installation folder. This keeps your planning files alongside your code.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use planning-with-files for any task requiring more than 5 tool calls, research projects, multi-step implementations, or anything requiring organization.

faq.question: What is the 2-action rule?
faq.answer: After every 2 view, browser, or search operations, immediately save key findings to files. This prevents visual and multimodal information from being lost.

faq.question: How does the 3-strike error protocol work?
faq.answer: Attempt 1: Diagnose and fix. Attempt 2: Try alternative approach. Attempt 3: Broader rethink. After 3 failures: escalate to user with what you tried.

faq.question: What is the read-before-decide pattern?
faq.answer: Before major decisions, read task_plan.md to bring goals back into your attention window. This prevents losing track of original objectives during long tasks.

faq.question: What is KV-cache and why does it matter?
faq.answer: KV-cache is a mechanism for caching computed tokens. A high hit rate reduces costs significantly (10x cheaper). Stable prompts maintain cache validity.
